text: Continuing Achilles tendon-loading activity (running and jumping) using a pain-monitoring model during rehabilitation produces no worse outcomes than stopping such activity for the first 6 weeks.

A graduated Achilles tendon-loading strengthening program significantly improves VISA-A-S scores from baseline (~57) to 12-month follow-up (~85-91) regardless of whether running/jumping is continued or paused initially.
A pain-monitoring model can be used to safely guide continued running and jumping during Achilles tendinopathy rehabilitation without demonstrable negative effects.
Complete rest from tendon-loading activities is not necessary during Achilles tendinopathy rehabilitation when a pain-monitoring model is used to regulate activity levels.
Significant improvements in Achilles tendinopathy symptoms can be observed as early as the first evaluation time point and are maintained through 12 months with a progressive tendon-loading program.
specific_value: P < .01 at all evaluation time points
